title: Assertion `memIsValid(&r.aMem[ii])' failed.
description:
yongheng added on 2020-03-15 21:01:52:
POC:
---
CREATE TABLE v0(v1);
CREATE TABLE v3(v5, v4);
INSERT INTO v0 VALUES(0);
CREATE INDEX v9 ON v3(v4, v4, v5);
SELECT v5 FROM v0 LEFT JOIN v3 ON v4 = NULL AND v5 IN(0);
---

It triggers an assertion in debug build of trunk.
drh added on 2020-03-16 03:19:13:
The assert() was identifying a reference to an uninitialized register
in the byte-code.  In production builds, uninitialized registers have a
value of NULL.  Whether or not this NULL changed the outcome of the
OP_IfNoHope opcode in which it occurred makes no difference in the final
output.  The correct answer is for the jump to be taken.  
But if the OP_IfNoHope falls through,
then the OP_IfNotOpen opcode that follows will jump to the same address.
The same result is obtained either way.
It is good to fix this problem, but the same (correct) answer is obtained
even without the fix.
The fix is to reverse the order of the two opcodes so that the
OP_IfNotOpen opcode occurs first.  The OP_IfNotOpen opcode will always be
taken and thus prevents the OP_IfNoHope opcode
from running when the register is uninitialized.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table1(col1 INTEGER, col2 TEXT, col3 REAL);
CREATE TABLE test_table2(colA SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, colB INTEGER, colC TEXT DEFAULT NULL);
SELECT colB FROM test_table2 LEFT JOIN test_table1 ON colB IS NULL AND col1 IN(1);
key_bug_features: SELECT with NULL condition and uninitialized register
